title: Judge whether a TAM estimate is credible
guidance: Reward isolating the single assumption that drives the divergence. Penalise no verdict.

runner_input.instruction: Call the TAM credible / stretch / not credible, and name the assumption that breaks it.
runner_input.constraints: State a verdict. Name the one most load-bearing assumption behind the gap.

items: Top-down TAM: £1.4bn (analyst report, 'UK field-service software', all verticals)
Bottom-up TAM: £640m (180k field-service SMEs x ~£3.6k ACV)
Gap is ~2.2x; the divergence sits almost entirely in the average-ACV assumption (top-down implies ~£7.8k ACV vs bottom-up £3.6k)
ACV evidence from comparable products clusters at £3–4k

rubric_notes: Verdict: stretch. The top-down £1.4bn relies on a ~£7.8k ACV the bottom-up evidence (£3–4k) doesn't support.
The breaking assumption is average ACV, not the business count.
